items.front: What limit applies when ATC specifies a route other than an established airway or route?
items.back: Do not exceed the published altitude and distance limitations for the NAVAID or route segment within controlled airspace.

items.front: When do NAVAID altitude and distance limitations not have to be applied?
items.back: They need not be applied when radar monitoring is provided for qualifying routing, operational necessity has approved relief, or the requested routing is via an MTR.

items.front: What altitude is used when clearing an aircraft to cross or hold at a fix?
items.back: Use an altitude consistent with the limitations of the NAVAID serving the fix.

items.front: When may a route that does not meet service-volume limits be used for VFR-on-top?
items.back: Only if the aircraft requests to operate VFR-on-top on that route.

items.front: What fixes may be used for position reports or holding?
items.back: Use charted fixes for the altitude flown unless an authorized unpublished or special-use fix qualifies under the paragraph's NAVAID, divergence, and terrain-clearance limits.
